top_stories.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
top_stories.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to mass harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign enabled token theft without deploying malware, representing a large-scale attack on authentication infrastructure affecting enterprise Office users globally.

top_stories.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
top_stories.summary: A threat group tracked as UNC6783 is conducting a corporate breach campaign by impersonating support staff and using fake Okta login pages to steal credentials and access corporate systems. The attackers combine social engineering with the fraudulent Okta authentication pages to exfiltrate sensitive data from targeted organizations.

top_stories.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
top_stories.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, a security feature that cryptographically binds browser sessions to a specific device to prevent session token theft. DBSC uses the device's TPM chip to tie authentication sessions to hardware, making stolen session cookies unusable on other devices.

top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the emergence of AI agents as highly privileged identities that expose limitations in traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls. The core argument centers on the inadequacy of standing privilege models for managing AI agent access, implying a need for just-in-time or ephemeral privilege approaches.

top_stories.title: Google Chrome 146 Adds Device Bound Session Credentials to Stop Session Cookie Theft on Windows
top_stories.summary: Google has introduced Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 for Windows, a security feature designed to prevent session cookie theft. DBSC cryptographically binds browser sessions to a specific device, making stolen session cookies unusable on other machines.

news.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
news.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws, making this a significant update for teams managing privileged access.

news.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
news.summary: APT41, a China-backed threat group, has deployed a new backdoor specifically designed to harvest credentials from major cloud platforms including AWS, Google Azure, and Alibaba. The group is using typosquatting techniques to obscure command-and-control communications while targeting cloud identity credentials.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the detection model shifts required to counter them. It focuses on how attackers are using legitimate credentials to blend in with normal activity, making identity-centric detection approaches critical for cybersecurity teams.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Russia's Fancy Bear APT group continues its global cyber espionage campaign targeting organizations of varying technical sophistication. Security experts are recommending patching and zero trust implementation as non-negotiable defenses against the threat actor.

news.title: Over 100 Chrome Web Store extensions steal user accounts, data
news.summary: Over 100 malicious Chrome Web Store extensions have been discovered stealing Google OAuth2 Bearer tokens alongside deploying backdoors and conducting ad fraud. The attack targets OAuth2 authentication tokens, which are used for identity verification and authorization across Google services and connected applications.

news.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
news.summary: Specops published an explainer on how Zero Trust architecture addresses identity security challenges, focusing on credential theft and privilege escalation. The piece covers five methods including identity-first access controls, device trust enforcement, and lateral movement prevention.

news.title: FBI takedown of W3LL phishing service leads to developer arrest
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian authorities dismantled the W3LL phishing platform and arrested its alleged developer in the first US-Indonesia coordinated enforcement action against a phishing kit developer. W3LL was a phishing-as-a-service platform known for its adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) capabilities that could bypass MFA protections, primarily targeting Microsoft 365 accounts.

news.title: Raspberry Pi OS 6.2 disables passwordless sudo by default
news.summary: Raspberry Pi OS 6.2 has disabled passwordless sudo by default for new installations as a security hardening measure. This changes the default privilege escalation behavior, requiring users to authenticate with a password when using sudo commands.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and disrupting infrastructure used to steal account credentials in fraud attempts exceeding $20 million. The W3LL toolkit was an off-the-shelf phishing kit specifically designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 business email accounts at scale.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real-time, acting as a man-in-the-middle relay that captures usernames, passwords, and MFA codes by forwarding credentials to legitimate sites. The service bypasses traditional phishing defenses by using the actual brand websites rather than static copies, effectively defeating MFA protections through adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) techniques.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: A new open-source tool called Prmana replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the Linux host via PAM, using DPoP (RFC 9449) for cryptographic proof of token possession. The tool is written in Rust, released under Apache 2.0, and integrates OIDC-based authentication directly into the Linux PAM stack for SSH access.

news.title: [Sponsor] WorkOS FGA: The Authorization Layer for AI Agents
news.summary: WorkOS has launched Fine-Grained Authorization (FGA), an authorization layer specifically designed for AI agents. The product targets machine-speed authorization requirements for agentic AI systems.

news.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
news.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity management as a unified governance challenge. The interview covers his perspective from experience at IBM and CyberArk on bringing visibility and governance to non-human identities.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, using hardware-based cryptographic keys to bind session cookies to specific devices on Windows. This feature directly counters infostealer malware that steals session cookies to bypass authentication, with significant implications for session management and MFA bypass attack vectors relevant to IAM practitioners.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping Device-Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks where infostealers extract and exfiltrate authentication cookies for account takeover. DBSC cryptographically binds session credentials to a specific device, rendering stolen cookies useless on attacker-controlled systems.

news.title: Google Chrome adds infostealer protection against session cookie theft
news.summary: Google has deployed Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 for Windows, a security feature that cryptographically binds session cookies to a specific device to prevent theft by infostealer malware. This directly addresses session hijacking attacks that bypass authentication controls, including MFA, by stealing valid session tokens.

news.title: 'Several dozen' high-value corporations hit by new extortion crew in helpdesk phishing spree
news.summary: A new extortion crew has compromised several dozen high-value corporations by exploiting helpdesk social engineering and phishing attacks, according to Google. The attacks target identity-related weaknesses by manipulating helpdesk staff to bypass authentication controls, a technique closely associated with MFA fatigue and vishing tactics previously used by groups like Scattered Spider.

news.title: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
news.summary: Microsoft DART researchers identified a financially motivated threat actor (Storm-2755) targeting Canadian employee accounts to gain unauthorized access and divert payroll. The attacks involve compromising employee identity accounts and manipulating access to HR/payroll systems, making it directly relevant to account security and access management practices.

news.title: When attackers already have the keys, MFA is just another door to open
news.summary: The article addresses how stolen credentials undermine MFA by turning authentication systems into attack surfaces, specifically discussing phishing relay and MFA bypass techniques. Token's wearable biometric authentication solution is presented as a method to verify the user rather than the session to counter these threats.
